# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# anti-pattern. This violates the Principle of Least Privilege and creates
# significant security risks including:
# - Full project compromise if the service account key is leaked
# - Ability to exfiltrate all data, delete all resources, modify IAM policies
# - No audit boundary - the account can cover its own tracks
# - Violation of most compliance frameworks (SOC2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, etc.)
#
# Instead, the template below grants only the specific roles typically needed
# for CI/CD infrastructure deployments. Add or remove roles based on your
# actual requirements.

resources:

- name: cicd-pipeline-service-account
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
    displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
    description: Service account for CI/CD pipeline with least-privilege permissions

# Grant only the specific roles the CI/CD pipeline actually needs
- name: cicd-binding-deployment-manager
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/deploymentmanager.editor
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-compute-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/compute.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-storage-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/storage.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-container-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/container.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-cloudsql-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/cloudsql.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-iam-serviceaccount-user
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-logging-writer
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/logging.logWriter
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

- name: cicd-binding-monitoring-editor
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/monitoring.editor
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount

# NOTE: If you encounter permission errors during deployment, identify the
# specific permission needed and add the narrowest role that includes it.
# Do NOT escalate to roles/owner or roles/editor.
# Reference: https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles

outputs:
- name: serviceAccountEmail
  value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)
- name: serviceAccountName
  value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.name)
